prompt: I'm designing an E2E journey for my React app. Users can sign up, create a post, and see it in the feed. That's three actions. Should I make it one journey or three separate journeys?
expected_output: Explain: one journey (one user goal: 'new user creates a post'). Precondition: no account. Actions: (1) sign up, (2) create post, (3) navigate to feed. Assertion: post visible in feed. It's atomic. If you split it into three journeys, you lose the end-to-end signal and can't catch the 'post created but not in feed' bug.
